method: SkillOpt-adapted assessment (triage, no full loop run)
assessment: Assessed as a scale-up candidate and triaged OUT: the proghg-submission skill already surfaces its operative facts (strands/routes, double-anonymized review, ORCID, COPE/ICMJE/AI disclosures, the no-empirical-work scope rule) and correctly hedges the genuinely volatile ones. Its source map confirms that the high-value numbers an author asks about — exact per-strand word limits, the abstract cap, current editors, the OA APC — are themselves 需复核/volatile, so there is no verified fact available to add. A SkillOpt edit here would have to either invent a volatile number (the gate would reject it, cf. HLR edit E2) or pad without adding verified content (no held-out improvement). Honest result: no high-value gated edit available; skill left unchanged.
